title: Moving Up (2)

jokes.text: Can't believe Michael Bay spent a billion dollars of his own money to build that for The Rock.
jokes.setup: Andy sees Alcatraz and mentions Michael Bay
jokes.punchline: Thinks Michael Bay personally built Alcatraz for the movie The Rock
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy misunderstanding that shows his childlike logic and pop culture obsession

jokes.text: I couldn't tell, 'cause you had that mask thing over your eyes.
jokes.setup: Andy asking if Ben was trying to sleep on the plane
jokes.punchline: Doesn't understand what a sleep mask is for
jokes.explanation: Mild Andy confusion moment, consistent with character but not exceptional

jokes.text: What happens at a National Parks Conference is compiled and emailed to you in a PDF the following Monday.
jokes.setup: Leslie starts with 'you know what they say' implying a common saying
jokes.punchline: Completely mundane bureaucratic reality instead of 'what happens in Vegas stays in Vegas'
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of expectations with perfect Leslie voice - bureaucratic and earnest

jokes.text: They give tours. Yeah. That's exactly how they'll be expecting me to try to break in.
jokes.setup: Ben suggests they take a tour of Alcatraz
jokes.punchline: Andy thinks taking a tour is somehow part of breaking in
jokes.explanation: Good Andy logic - overthinking in a completely wrong direction

jokes.text: Wow. I've never worked hard on anything. What a cool life!
jokes.setup: Tom reflects on working hard on his restaurant
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's never worked hard before and finds this delightful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - shallow but self-aware about his laziness

jokes.text: From now on, everyone call me Kristen. Because I am wigging out right now!
jokes.setup: Tom realizes they have to open tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Makes elaborate pun about wigging out/wearing a wig named Kristen
jokes.explanation: Convoluted Tom wordplay that's more clever than funny

jokes.text: They're not even done painting that sign. It says 'Tom's bi.'
jokes.setup: Restaurant isn't ready to open
jokes.punchline: Sign painter hasn't finished, creating unfortunate partial message
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag with good setup and economical delivery

jokes.text: Actually, no, that's good.
jokes.setup: Just learned his sign says 'Tom's bi'
jokes.punchline: Immediately decides this is actually positive for business
jokes.explanation: Quick character beat showing Tom's opportunistic mindset

jokes.text: ...rise from hell fire!
jokes.setup: Team chant about McDonald's
jokes.punchline: Craig adds dramatic demonic ending to innocent restaurant chant
jokes.explanation: Classic Craig over-the-top moment that escalates beyond reason

jokes.text: That's mine. Sorry, Lester. Yours is right here.
jokes.setup: Mix-up at a fancy government lunch
jokes.punchline: Awkward correction showing bureaucratic formality
jokes.explanation: Minor cringe moment that establishes bureaucratic setting

jokes.text: The Department of the Interior's resident bad boy?
jokes.setup: Grant mentions Liam Bonneville
jokes.punchline: Leslie calls a government bureaucrat a 'bad boy'
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her government nerd enthusiasm

jokes.text: No idea.
jokes.setup: Ben asks what Gryzzl does
jokes.punchline: Andy admits he has no clue about the company they're pitching to
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy honesty - completely unprepared but confident

jokes.text: Six months ago, I was working in a taco truck. Now, I own one-third of the Portland Trailblazers.
jokes.setup: Tech company success story
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous leap from taco truck to NBA ownership
jokes.explanation: Good satire of tech industry excess and instant wealth

jokes.text: In fact, a lot of people go to the library just to order pizza online.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to impress with Pawnee's online pizza ordering
jokes.punchline: Reveals people only use library computers for pizza
jokes.explanation: Good sad truth about Pawnee's priorities and digital divide

jokes.text: Sure, obviously, we're no Akron. I mean, we're more like Dayton. But with your help, we can become Toledo.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to sell Pawnee to tech company
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly unimpressive Ohio cities as aspirational goals
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating desperation with specific geographic comedy

jokes.text: So instead, let me say, 'Nah, bro.'
jokes.setup: Claims they don't say 'no' because it's counterproductive
jokes.punchline: Says 'nah, bro' instead, which is the same thing
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate double-speak and bro culture

jokes.text: Oh, wow, the stripes are so wavy. It's really...
jokes.setup: Tom pretending to appreciate Ron's woodwork
jokes.punchline: Clearly has no idea what he's talking about and trails off
jokes.explanation: Tom's fake appreciation for craftsmanship he doesn't understand

jokes.text: Who do you think I am, Thomas Hucker?
jokes.setup: Tom demanding chairs faster
jokes.punchline: Ron references obscure furniture maker as if everyone should know
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - obscure craftsmanship reference delivered with authority

jokes.text: I've never asked you for anything today.
jokes.setup: Tom pleading with Ron for help
jokes.punchline: Technically true but completely misses the point of the phrase
jokes.explanation: Clever literal interpretation of common phrase shows Tom's opportunistic thinking

jokes.text: I cut out your face and made it look like you were dunking over Charles Barkley.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her childhood poster of Rebecca
jokes.punchline: Elaborate photo manipulation of bureaucrat dunking on NBA legend
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Leslie fangirl behavior with specific visual comedy

jokes.text: It wasn't bad.
jokes.setup: Leslie's famous river cleanup proposal being discussed
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement from government bureaucrat
jokes.explanation: Classic bureaucratic understatement that establishes character

jokes.text: I didn't take any photos. What? Grant, come on!
jokes.setup: Leslie had amazing lunch with heroes
jokes.punchline: Grant's practical oversight ruins her documentation obsession
jokes.explanation: Leslie's need to document everything clashes with reality

jokes.text: You are from Chicago, so you like it!
jokes.setup: Meeting Michelle Obama who mentions Chicago
jokes.punchline: Leslie's nervously obvious statement of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Leslie's starstruck nervous energy leads to stating obvious facts

jokes.text: I agree with you on all things. Throughout history. Until the end of time, forever.
jokes.setup: Leslie meeting Michelle Obama
jokes.punchline: Escalates agreement to cosmic proportions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie escalation showing her overwhelming enthusiasm and fangirl energy

jokes.text: I'm getting louder and now I can't stop! My apologies!
jokes.setup: Leslie getting excited meeting Michelle Obama
jokes.punchline: Self-aware about losing volume control but can't stop
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment - self-aware but unable to control her excitement

jokes.text: I thought he was gonna be on it.
jokes.setup: Andy and Ben watching something go down a hill
jokes.punchline: Andy expected Ben to ride whatever they sent down
jokes.explanation: Andy's dangerous assumption about what they were doing

jokes.text: What? Why didn't you lead with that?
jokes.setup: Leslie casually mentions meeting Michelle Obama
jokes.punchline: Ben's incredulity at her casual delivery of huge news
jokes.explanation: Good straight man reaction to Leslie's priority confusion

jokes.text: I don't know! Liam Bonneville has me in a tizzy!
jokes.setup: Ben asking why she didn't lead with meeting Michelle Obama
jokes.punchline: Leslie more excited about obscure bureaucrat than First Lady
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - government nerd crushes trump celebrity encounters

jokes.text: This is not government work. As such, I treat it with care and attention.
jokes.setup: Tom asking Ron to work faster on chairs
jokes.punchline: Ron's backhanded slam at government work quality
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron philosophy delivered with perfect deadpan authority

jokes.text: No. Can't I just stay in the back and spit in people's food like we planned?
jokes.setup: Tom asking April to handle service
jokes.punchline: April reveals they apparently planned food contamination
jokes.explanation: Perfect April darkness - treats food service as warfare

jokes.text: My sinuses are on the fritz, and smell is 90% of taste! Maybe 95!
jokes.setup: Craig complaining about sawdust affecting his senses
jokes.punchline: Escalates his own percentage estimate in real time
jokes.explanation: Craig's dramatic overreaction with escalating made-up statistics

jokes.text: Fine, but if he raises his voice once, once, I'm out.
jokes.setup: Donna agreeing to work with Craig
jokes.punchline: Sets impossible standard for Craig who always yells
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Donna's standards vs Craig's nature

jokes.text: No, it's my dog's rectum.
jokes.setup: Tom asking about awful pictures on menus
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact explanation makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry disaster with perfect gross-out timing

jokes.text: Lord Sheldon has a growth, so we're sending pictures over to the vet.
jokes.setup: Explaining the dog rectum pictures
jokes.punchline: Makes the situation even more inappropriate for restaurant menus
jokes.explanation: Larry's innocent explanation makes the restaurant disaster worse

jokes.text: This is the worst thing you've ever done.
jokes.setup: Larry's menu disaster with dog photos
jokes.punchline: Tom's dramatic overstatement of Larry's long history of mistakes
jokes.explanation: Tom's dramatic reaction acknowledges Larry's consistent incompetence

jokes.text: It was too perfect. It looked machine-made.
jokes.setup: Tom asking why Ron rejected a chair
jokes.punchline: Ron's impossible perfectionist standards for handmade items
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron craftsmanship philosophy - rejecting perfection for being too perfect

jokes.text: Ooh, we could tell them that there's buried treasure in Pawnee, and then, after they give us the free WiFi, we tell them that the treasure was love all along.
jokes.setup: Andy suggesting how to convince Gryzzl
jokes.punchline: Elaborate con ending with cheesy moral lesson
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy plan - starts criminal, ends wholesome, completely impractical

jokes.text: Every time someone in Pawnee clicks through a slideshow of American Music Award red carpet sideboob fails, they'll say, 'Thank you, Ben Wyatt.'
jokes.setup: Ben's vision for his WiFi legacy
jokes.punchline: Specific lowbrow internet content as his monument
jokes.explanation: Good Ben moment - idealistic goals meet internet reality

jokes.text: Are they throwing dirt into a fan?
jokes.setup: Andy wondering if they're playing his other games
jokes.punchline: Reveals another terrible game concept
jokes.explanation: Good Andy game design - impractical and potentially dangerous

jokes.text: The key is, you have to throw the dirt into the back of the fan.
jokes.setup: Explaining his dirt-throwing game
jokes.punchline: Adds specific technique that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Andy's nonsensical game rules that sound authoritative

jokes.text: You're The Architect? Yeah, right. And I'm the alchemist of the Hinterlands.
jokes.setup: Ben claiming he invented Cones of Dunshire
jokes.punchline: Mocks Ben with made-up game character title
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Ben to prove his game knowledge

jokes.text: There can't be an alchemist of the Hinterlands. The Hinterlands is a shadow kingdom that can only sustain a provost or a denier.
jokes.setup: Executive mocking Ben with fake game title
jokes.punchline: Ben's detailed correction proves his expertise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben nerd moment - obscure game rules delivered with authority

jokes.text: Neither. I'm the maverick.
jokes.setup: Asked to choose between corporal or warrior
jokes.punchline: Ben chooses maverick, showing mastery of the game
jokes.explanation: Ben's confident game choice shows his strategic thinking

jokes.text: You know when it was completed? Last month.
jokes.setup: Rebecca asking about merger completion timeline
jokes.punchline: Reveals 9-year delay from 2004 to last month
jokes.explanation: Excellent timing reveal that deflates Leslie's confidence

jokes.text: You lost your cityhood?
jokes.setup: Rebecca explaining merger disasters
jokes.punchline: Leslie's horror at losing municipal status
jokes.explanation: Leslie's worst nightmare - bureaucratic dissolution

jokes.text: Which is it? Fruity or earthy? It can't be both, they're mutually exclusive!
jokes.setup: Donna describing wine as fruity and earthy
jokes.punchline: Craig's dramatic wine expertise meltdown
jokes.explanation: Craig's food service perfectionism creating restaurant chaos

jokes.text: A man without a palate isn't a man.
jokes.setup: Craig's wine crisis continues
jokes.punchline: Dramatic existential statement about taste
jokes.explanation: Classic Craig melodrama - makes taste about masculinity and existence

jokes.text: We're having a moment, you monster!
jokes.setup: Tom interrupting Craig and Donna
jokes.punchline: Craig's dramatic defense of their wine crisis bonding
jokes.explanation: Craig treating restaurant emergency as romantic drama

jokes.text: Ron, you're amazing. You've always been like a grandpa to me.
jokes.setup: Tom praising Ron's work ethic
jokes.punchline: Accidentally calls Ron old while trying to compliment
jokes.explanation: Tom's inadvertent insult while trying to be grateful

jokes.text: Restaurant superstition, breaking a glass on the first night is good luck.
jokes.setup: Glass breaks during restaurant opening
jokes.punchline: Tom improvises fake superstition to save face
jokes.explanation: Tom's quick thinking to turn disaster into positive

jokes.text: You forgot about the essence of the game. It's about the cones.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining his winning strategy
jokes.punchline: Reveals the obvious game title element as secret strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben game designer moment - overthinking leads to obvious answer

jokes.text: I move my abbot to the ocean hex, which moves my brinksman to the devil's lair, and pushes my farmer, yes, my humble farmer, directly into the central cone.
jokes.setup: Ben executing his winning move
jokes.punchline: Complex chain reaction ending with humble farmer winning
jokes.explanation: Ben's elaborate game mechanics with dramatic humble farmer victory

jokes.text: Yeah, man! We did it! This whole company is ours!
jokes.setup: Ben wins the game
jokes.punchline: Andy thinks winning a game means they own the company
jokes.explanation: Andy's misunderstanding of stakes and consequences

jokes.text: Spaghetti, linguine, fettuccine, ravioli, rigatoni, Ferrari, Lamborghini, bucatini, lasagna.
jokes.setup: April listing pasta types from memory
jokes.punchline: Slips in car brands that sound Italian
jokes.explanation: Perfect April sabotage - subtle, clever, and completely in character

jokes.text: We had to throw our menus away because they are covered with pictures of Larry's dog's rectum.
jokes.setup: Customer asks for menu
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact explanation of the disaster
jokes.explanation: April's deadpan delivery makes the gross situation even funnier

jokes.text: Great news. Lord Sheldon's gonna make it. Yeah. We just have to apply a salve to his anus every hour for the rest of his life, but... Oh! Enjoy your meal!
jokes.setup: Larry getting good news about his dog
jokes.punchline: Graphic medical details delivered to restaurant customers
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete obliviousness to appropriate dining conversation

jokes.text: You have no idea how to run a restaurant. I've been in this game before, and I can see the cracks starting to form.
jokes.setup: Restaurant soft opening going badly
jokes.punchline: Tom's father delivering harsh but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Dr. Saperstein's brutal honesty about Tom's failing business

jokes.text: I'm gonna be paralyzed by hypotheticals until I die here, in this mini van! At the San Francisco airport.
jokes.setup: Leslie can't decide about the job
jokes.punchline: Dramatic death scenario in specific mundane location
jokes.explanation: Leslie's melodrama with specific mundane details

jokes.text: I love quitting. When I was a kid and things didn't go my way, I would just take my ball and go home. That's better than winning, 'cause then, your friends can't play anymore!
jokes.setup: Ron's work philosophy about finishing jobs
jokes.punchline: Tom's childhood strategy was actively ruining fun for others
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom origin story - selfish from childhood with twisted logic

jokes.text: Well, I hate doing work, but I love being flattered, so maybe I'll give it another try.
jokes.setup: Tom deciding whether to quit
jokes.punchline: Honest about his motivations being purely ego-driven
jokes.explanation: Tom's refreshing honesty about his shallow motivations

jokes.text: Because you currently owe me $16,000. For the wood. What?
jokes.setup: Ron giving Tom encouragement
jokes.punchline: Reveals massive hidden debt for the chairs
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and escalation - Ron's support has ulterior financial motive

jokes.text: These are the redwoods George Lucas used to create the forest moon of Endor. Sorry, that's not why we're here. Let's go.
jokes.setup: Ben taking Leslie somewhere meaningful
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by Star Wars trivia then refocuses
jokes.explanation: Ben's nerd knowledge competing with romantic mission

jokes.text: Hi, Grant. I want the job. I have a lot of questions, but I'm a little amped up right now, so I'll probably just hang up.
jokes.setup: Leslie making important career call
jokes.punchline: Self-aware about being too excited to handle conversation
jokes.explanation: Leslie's self-awareness about her excitement overwhelming her

jokes.text: No, I don't. I'm sorry. That was weird. Okay, bye. Oh, this is Lester Kanopf. No, you know... Okay. Bye.
jokes.setup: Leslie's excited phone call continues
jokes.punchline: Accidentally says 'I love you' then makes it worse with wrong name
jokes.explanation: Leslie's excitement leads to increasingly awkward phone mistakes

jokes.text: All right, so we got a four-way going with my ex, my brother, and this rando? Okay, if that's where the night is going.
jokes.setup: Tom asking for help at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio immediately assumes sexual scenario
jokes.explanation: Classic Jean-Ralphio misinterpretation in most inappropriate direction

jokes.text: Come on, Ron. It's gonna be so fun. Thank you, Andrew. But fun or not, playing music is something I like to keep private, along with my family, my conversations, and my whereabouts at all times.
jokes.setup: Andy asking Ron to perform as Duke Silver
jokes.punchline: Ron's comprehensive list of privacy preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron privacy obsession escalating to absurd levels

jokes.text: Your mission, should you choose to accept it, is to accept the following missions.
jokes.setup: Andy giving Mission Impossible style briefing
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Andy's attempt at spy movie dialogue creates logical nonsense

jokes.text: Their first project will be to come up with a shorter name for the group.
jokes.setup: Announcing the very long volunteer group name
jokes.punchline: Admits the name is too long and needs shortening
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about bureaucratic naming conventions

jokes.text: You're right, the name is too long, I was picturing bigger hats.
jokes.setup: Andy saying the volunteer group name is too long
jokes.punchline: Leslie's solution is larger hats to fit more text
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie solution - ignore practical problem, expand physical medium

jokes.text: Our most faithful citizen. She never abandoned us.
jokes.setup: Statue honoring Leslie's faithfulness
jokes.punchline: Ironic timing as she's about to leave town
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - monument to loyalty unveiled as she leaves

jokes.text: Only a moron would ever live anywhere other than Pawnee, Indiana. Leslie Knope, multiple occasions.
jokes.setup: Reading Leslie's quote on the statue
jokes.punchline: Her past anti-leaving statements now seem hypocritical
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony with quotable Leslie hyperbole coming back to haunt her

jokes.text: Leslie, don't cry. It's not that great. Doesn't even look like you. Or actually, you know what? It kinda does.
jokes.setup: Leslie crying at her statue
jokes.punchline: Ron's failed attempt at comfort makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Ron's social awkwardness making emotional moment worse

jokes.text: I ordered a small cheeseburger and both the buns were pizzas.
jokes.setup: Band member praising Pawnee
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous food combination as evidence of town's greatness
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Pawnee food innovation that sounds both disgusting and amazing

jokes.text: Judge gave me two ankle bracelets. Said it was the first time he ever had to do that. Ja boy's a question on the bar exam! Ya heard!
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio explaining his house arrest
jokes.punchline: Proud of creating legal precedent for double ankle bracelets
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jean-Ralphio moment - turning criminal excess into bragging rights

jokes.text: What is that? Just general rock, man. It's gonna be fun. She's my cherry pie Oh, yeah, yeah, yeah
jokes.setup: Jamm describes his terrible cover band
jokes.punchline: He demonstrates with awful singing
jokes.explanation: Standard Jamm terribleness, shows his complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Damn, Donna, why you gotta bring the Quackson Five into this?
jokes.setup: Donna threatening to reveal embarrassing story about rubber ducks
jokes.punchline: Ginuwine's pun name for his childhood duck collection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pun combining Jackson 5 with rubber ducks, perfectly delivered

jokes.text: Now if you'll excuse me, there's a booth over there serving something called 'fried sausage quilts,' so I'm going to buy the booth.
jokes.setup: Ron declining Leslie's job offer
jokes.punchline: Absurd food item makes him want to buy entire booth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron food obsession with absurd fried item and extreme reaction

jokes.text: No, just trolling for some daddy. Sorry, I pronounced that wrong. I'm trolling for some dad-dy.
jokes.setup: Tammy explaining why she's at children's event
jokes.punchline: Emphasis change doesn't actually improve the inappropriate statement
jokes.explanation: Classic Tammy inappropriate comment with fake correction that doesn't help

jokes.text: I will defeat you right into my pants.
jokes.setup: Tammy threatening Ron
jokes.punchline: Confusing threat that doesn't make sense
jokes.explanation: Tammy's nonsensical sexual threat shows her desperation

jokes.text: I always figured we'd, you know, whatever, break up because I got drafted by the Colts. And then in the first game, of course, I break my leg. The crowd starts chanting my name, and I give them the thumbs up. And then I go to the hospital, I get really good at chess, I end up beating the computer finally. And then the band just sort of drifts apart.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining how he thought Mouse Rat would end
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy involving NFL career, injury, chess mastery, and band dissolution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy fantasy with random but specific details that somehow make sense for him

jokes.text: One kid peed his pants because he didn't want to miss anything. What? I gave a kid pee-pants? Yes! That's why I do what I do.
jokes.setup: April describing Andy's performance success
jokes.punchline: Andy's proud reaction to causing involuntary urination
jokes.explanation: Andy's pure joy at achieving ultimate entertainer goal

jokes.text: Wait, what?
jokes.setup: Andy mentions losing April's cell phone in ball pit
jokes.punchline: April's delayed reaction to buried information
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat showing Andy's casual relationship with other people's property

jokes.text: Chicago seems like there's a lot of stuff to do and people, but I like to do nothing with no one. So, thank you and I love you, but no thank you, and I hate you. More I love you.
jokes.setup: April rejecting Leslie's job offer
jokes.punchline: Contradictory statements that somehow express her feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect April logic - contradictory but somehow emotionally coherent

jokes.text: Money, please! Money, please! Money, money, money!
jokes.setup: Dr. Saperstein arriving at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Immediately demanding money like a child
jokes.explanation: Dr. Saperstein's childish greed on full display

jokes.text: I'll have the 'Joan.' It's a tumbler of gin and it's got crushed aspirin around the rim.
jokes.setup: Joan ordering a drink named after herself
jokes.punchline: Cocktail recipe suggests depression and self-medication
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about celebrity lifestyle and pain management

jokes.text: Come work for us at the accounting firm? Okay. No, sorry, I can't. Wow. Shortest one yet.
jokes.setup: Barney asking Ben to work at accounting firm
jokes.punchline: Ben's instant rejection and Barney's tracking of rejection speed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ben's consistent rejection and Barney's sad persistence

jokes.text: Let's get divorced. That way we can get married again! Yes! Let's do it. You want to? Yes! We should do that, right? Yeah, totally. We're getting divorced! Yes!
jokes.setup: Andy wishing he could marry April again
jokes.punchline: Couple excitedly planning divorce to remarry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy and April logic - turning divorce into romantic gesture

jokes.text: And I've been to Dallas.
jokes.setup: Dr. Saperstein praising the Italian food
jokes.punchline: Uses Dallas as his reference point for Italian cuisine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Saperstein cultural reference that misses the mark entirely

jokes.text: Excuse me, Miss Hanley? Do you mind if I snap a 'You-ie'? It's what I call selfies of other people.
jokes.setup: Tom asking for photo with Kay Hanley
jokes.punchline: Made-up term that doesn't make sense
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom made-up slang that sounds cool but is logically flawed

jokes.text: You will not, no.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying she won't find better breakfast than JJ's Diner
jokes.punchline: Ron's absolute certainty delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron food authority delivered with absolute confidence

jokes.text: With your courage and small frame, you'd make an excellent coal miner.
jokes.setup: Ron suggesting alternative careers for Leslie
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment suggesting dangerous underground work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron compliment that's technically positive but completely inappropriate

jokes.text: Also, can you bring back Power Rangers? I don't know what it is you do, but you seem important enough to get that done.
jokes.setup: Grant addressing the group about Leslie's proposal
jokes.punchline: Random Power Rangers request based on assumed government power
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of bureaucratic assumptions about government power

jokes.text: Ed, you're a nice guy, but you're the most incompetent person I've ever worked with. And that includes Terry. Jeez. Get it together, Terry.
jokes.setup: Leslie firing Ed for incompetence
jokes.punchline: Terry taking it personally despite not being fired
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing workplace dynamics and Terry's insecurity

jokes.text: Ben, how many bags of marshmallows do I give them? None. Just let April do everything.
jokes.setup: Andy asking about feeding the kids
jokes.punchline: Ben's practical parenting vs Andy's chaotic approach
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between responsible Ben and chaos-enabling Andy

insight: Season finale leans on character comfort over comedy peaks at 77.8.
